title: Nigeria’s total debt stock rise to N24.9tn in first quarter of 2019 - FinancialEDGE
description: Nigeria’s total public debt rose to N24.947 trillion as of March 31, from N24.387 trillion as of December 31, 2018, growing marginally by 2.30 per cent, the Debt Management Office said. A statement issued by the office on Wednesday July 10, 2019, in Abuja said that the debt stock comprises of domestic and external debts
html:
Nigeria’s total public debt rose to N24.947 trillion as of March 31, from N24.387 trillion as of December 31, 2018, growing marginally by 2.30 per cent, the Debt Management Office said. A statement issued by the office on Wednesday July 10, 2019, in Abuja said that the debt stock comprises of domestic and external debts of the Federal Government, the 36 States and the Federal Capital Territory.

It added that the debt which rose by N560 billion was accounted for largely by domestic debt which grew by N458.36 billion, while external debt also increased by N101.64 billion during the same period. In relation to the Debt Management Strategy, the ratio of domestic to external debt stood at 68.49 per cent to 31.51 per cent at the end of March.

“The total public debt to Gross Domestic Product ratio was 19.03 per cent which is within the 25 per cent debt limit imposed by the government.”  The debt portfolio which was obtained from the DMO website showed that the Federal Government presently owes N13.1 trillion domestically, while the states and the FCT owe N3.97 trillion.
